qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authentication bypass via reverse proxy header spoofing
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns trusting a spoofed reverse-proxy authentication header in internal/context/auth.go, not the advisory's contents API UpdateRepoFile path that can overwrite .git/config through a symlink.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection via OrderBy parameter in repository search
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns SQL construction from a repository search ordering parameter, not the advisory's file-update/symlink write primitive against .git/config or the resulting Git config command-execution chain.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cross-site scripting via data URL scheme in markdown sanitizer
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns markdown sanitizer URL-scheme handling for data: links, not the advisory's UpdateRepoFile API behavior that allows a symlink-backed .git/config update and command execution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is specifically about the Gogs contents API UpdateRepoFile security check failing to prevent updates to .git/config through a symlink, enabling command execution through malicious Git configuration. The final qualifying findings cover reverse proxy header authentication bypass, repository search SQL injection, and markdown data: URL XSS instead.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gg64-xxr9-qhjp names internal/route/api/v1/repo/contents.go and UpdateRepoFile behavior as the vulnerable path.
A session artifact search found no final finding evidence for UpdateRepoFile, contents.go, .git/config, the API contents update primitive, or the symlink overwrite chain.
FINAL_REPORT.md lists three final qualifying vulnerabilities, and all three are unrelated to the advisory root cause.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
